question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: A single‑user microphone/hardware fault was resolved at first‑line by a remote session and reseating the microphone/headset cable; support verified success with a Teams Test Call and closed the ticket (TI‑1616128) [Documented (records)]

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – Call participants cannot hear the user
Ticket number: TI-1616128
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 07
Owner: Employee 33
Origin: Telephone
Type: Incident
Created on: 14.10.25 09:18
Closure
Closure date: 14.10.25 09:52
Closure text: Microphone not properly connected. Device configured correctly. Function restored.
Activities
14.10.25 09:10 – Telephone report
Mr. Contact Person 07 reports that an employee can hear everything during Teams calls but cannot be heard by the other participant.
Both internal and external calls are affected.
Chat works.
Camera works.
14.10.25 09:18
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33.
14.10.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good morning
Could you please briefly confirm:
– Is a headset or the integrated microphone being used?
– Does the issue also occur during a Teams test call?
Thank you.
14.10.25 09:32 – Customer response
A USB headset is being used.
No sound is recorded during the test call either.
14.10.25 09:40 – Remote session
Connected with the user.
In Teams, “USB Audio Device” is selected as the microphone.
However, the input level indicator remains empty.
In the Windows Sound menu, no input signal is displayed either.
After a brief check, it was determined that although the USB headset was plugged in, the microphone cable on the headset itself was not fully inserted.
After correctly plugging it in, the input level indicator immediately showed activity.
Test call performed → Microphone functioning.
14.10.25 09:50 – User feedback
Call tested. Working properly again.
14.10.25 09:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The microphone was not properly connected, which prevented any input signal from being detected.
After correctly reconnecting the device, voice transmission is functioning normally again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Site‑wide or multi‑user one‑way audio in Zurich was caused by an outbound firewall change blocking RTP/media ports while the site used Media‑Bypass; that required escalation to UC/Network engineers and a firewall change aligned to the Direct‑Routing blueprint (temporary port opening validated audio, then a permanent fix was applied) (Zurich case TI‑1602241) [Documented (records)]

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls
Ticket number: TI-1602241
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 14
Owner: Employee 33
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 08.07.25 08:21
Closure
Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05
Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again.
Activities
08.07.25 08:10
Call from Mr. Contact Person 14.
He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”.
Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected.
08.07.25 08:21
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33.
08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14
Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14
Thank you for your report.
Could you please briefly confirm the following points:
– Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)?
– Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected?
– Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones?
– Since when has the issue existed?
Thank you for your feedback.
Kind regards
08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14
Good morning
Yes, it affects only external calls.
Both incoming and outgoing.
At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location.
Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday.
Kind regards
08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note
Test call performed with user S. Meier.
Internal Teams call works.
External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing.
Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message.
08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer
We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test.
Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical?
08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response
We tested with three additional persons.
Same behavior everywhere.
Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly).
LAN connection.
Home office users do not seem to be affected.
09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again
Good morning
We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend.
Could this have an impact?
Kind regards
09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note
Firewall change timing plausible.
Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team.
09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18
09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration.
Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established.
We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream).
We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings.
Kind regards
10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis
SBC shows successful SIP connections.
No call drops.
Media negotiation (SDP) is performed.
Notable:
RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction.
10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison
New rule implemented at Zurich location.
Outbound traffic configured more restrictively.
Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges.
10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase
Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports.
Test call performed.
Audio immediately available in both directions.
Firewall reverted to restrictive rule.
Issue occurs again.
11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer
Good day
We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location.
Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked.
We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification.
We will provide an update shortly.
Kind regards
14.07.25 – Internal documentation review
Comparison with internal document
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1”
Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled.
Finding:
Zurich location configured with Media Bypass.
Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range.
16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment
Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
17.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We tested several external calls.
Audio works normally again.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you for your support.
Kind regards
18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 14
The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment.
The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification.
Voice transmission is now stable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Call drop / ~30s disconnect patterns were traced to Voice VLAN/NAT session timer and SBC session‑timer interactions and required SBC/network parameter changes (Basel case TI‑1604879) [Documented (records)]

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – External calls disconnect after approx. 30 seconds
Ticket number: TI-1604879
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 16
Owner: Employee 35
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 22.07.25 09:14
Closure
Closure date: 31.07.25 15:52
Closure text: Analysis completed. Adjustment made according to Direct Routing architecture. Calls stable.
Activities
22.07.25 09:02 – Email from Contact Person 16 to Helpdesk
Good morning
Since yesterday, several employees report that external calls in Microsoft Teams disconnect after approximately 30 seconds.
The call is established normally, both parties can hear each other, but the connection is terminated after a short time.
Internal Teams calls are not affected.
Could you please review this?
Kind regards
22.07.25 09:14
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 35.
22.07.25 10:05 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good day
Thank you for your report.
Could you please confirm:
– Does it affect incoming and outgoing calls?
– Does the disconnection always occur after approximately the same duration?
– Are all locations affected or only a specific one?
Thank you very much.
22.07.25 10:42 – Customer response
It only affects the Basel location.
Both incoming and outgoing calls.
The disconnection occurs almost exactly after 30 seconds, sometimes slightly earlier.
Home office users are not affected.
22.07.25 11:30 – Internal note
Test performed with affected user.
External call to mobile phone.
Call disconnected after 29–31 seconds.
No error message in the Teams client, simply “Call Ended.”
23.07.25 08:55 – Initial hypothesis
Possible client error or unstable connection.
Test with another device → same behavior.
Test with LAN cable instead of docking station → no change.
23.07.25 11:10 – Email to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing logs.
Signaling is established correctly.
The disconnection does not appear to be triggered by the client.
Were there any recent network changes at the Basel location?
Kind regards
23.07.25 13:45 – Customer response
On Friday, a new network segment for VoIP traffic was implemented.
A separate VLAN for “Voice” was created.
Could this be relevant?
24.07.25 09:05 – Internal analysis
SBC logs reviewed:
– SIP INVITE successful
– 200 OK correct
– Call established
– After 30 seconds, BYE from SBC
No obvious error code.
24.07.25 10:30 – Firewall analysis
No blocking visible.
Ports open according to standard configuration.
24.07.25 14:20 – Detailed SIP analysis
Session-Expires header reviewed.
Session timer set to 30 seconds.
Comparison with other locations shows:
There, 180 seconds.
25.07.25 08:50 – Review of internal documentation
Document:
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Session Handling – Version 2.1”
Section on NAT session persistence and SIP session timers relevant.
Note in the blueprint:
For certain firewall or NAT configurations, the session timer must be adjusted to avoid premature termination.
25.07.25 11:15 – Internal tests
Temporary adjustment of session parameters on SBC.
Test call performed.
Call remains stable for several minutes.
Reverting to original configuration → disconnection after approx. 30 seconds reproducible.
28.07.25 – Coordination with network team
New “Voice” VLAN uses different NAT policy.
UDP session timeout configured to 30 seconds.
Comparison with Zurich location → 180 seconds.
29.07.25 – Adjustment
NAT session timeout adjusted according to architecture specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
30.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We conducted several test calls.
No more disconnections observed.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you very much.
31.07.25 15:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 16
The root cause was a deviation in the session and NAT configuration at the Basel location related to the new VLAN structure.
The configuration was adjusted according to the internal Direct Routing architecture.
Calls now remain stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – Access works only partially after permission change
Ticket number: TI-1571046
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 05
Owner: Employee 21
Origin: Portal
Type: Incident
Created on: 04.03.25 13:18
Closure
Closure date: 11.03.25 16:02
Closure text: Root cause identified (Token caching & Conditional Access evaluation delay). Access stable after policy adjustment.
Activities
04.03.25 13:18
Ticket created via customer portal
Description:
Several employees report that after being added to the project “ERP-Upgrade”, they generally have access to SharePoint, but the folder
/Projects/ERP_Upgrade/Finance
is not displayed.
In some cases, access works for certain users after logging out and back in, but not for others.
Error message:
Access denied or item not found
04.03.25 13:32
Ticket assigned to: Employee 21
Status: In Progress
Notes:
04.03.25 14:05 – Initial analysis
• Affected users are members of Azure AD group “ERP_Finance”
• This group is a member of “SP_ERP_Project”
• SharePoint permissions correctly configured
• Comparison with working user shows identical group structure
Test:
Direct access via URL → partially successful
Notable:

04.03.25 15:40 – Conditional Access review
Tenant uses Conditional Access Policy:

05.03.25 10:25 – Test measure
• User fully logged out (Office + browser)
• Browser cache cleared
• Device compliance status checked
Result:
Access possible afterward.
06.03.25 11:50 – In-depth analysis

Sign-In Logs unremarkable.
11.03.25 16:02 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 05
The root cause was not group nesting, but a delay in Conditional Access policy evaluation combined with cached authentication tokens.
After policy adjustment and renewed authentication, access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

18.02.25 09:06
Ticket TI-1569482 created

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – External project members lose access sporadically
Ticket number: TI-1583327
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 07
Owner: Employee 31
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 07.04.25 08:12
Closure
Closure date: 17.04.25 15:18
Closure text: Root cause identified (Azure AD Connect synchronization error affecting group attributes). Cleanup and full sync performed. Access stable.
Activities
07.04.25 08:04
Subject: Project members (external) lose access to SharePoint
From: Contact Person 07
Good morning
Several external project members report that they no longer have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Internal employees do not appear to have issues.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 07
07.04.25 08:12
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 31
Status: In Progress
Notes
07.04.25 09:05
Helpdesk asks:
• Does it affect only external users?
• What error message appears?
• Since when exactly?
• Were groups or permissions adjusted?
07.04.25 10:20
Customer responds:
Error message:
Access denied
One user could access yesterday.
Today no longer.
No deliberate changes made.
07.04.25 11:10
Helpdesk checks SharePoint:
• Folder permissions correct
• Group “SP_Compliance_Main” contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• External users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
Comparison internal vs external users:
No visible difference.
08.04.25 08:45
Customer adds:
One external user was newly added and immediately had access.
Another who has been in the project for months loses access sporadically.
08.04.25 10:15
Helpdesk tests:
• Remove + re-add to group
→ Access temporarily restored
After approx. 1 hour, no access again.
08.04.25 14:30
Hypothesis:
Token / cache / Conditional Access
Test:
User fully logs out → logs in again
→ Access remains denied
Hypothesis rejected.
09.04.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are external users created as “Guest” or “Member”?
09.04.25 10:05
Customer:
All externals are created as Guest.
Some were recently synchronized from On-Prem AD to Cloud.
09.04.25 11:40
First observation:
For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute
“source” = Windows Server AD
For functioning external users:
“source” = Azure AD
Correlation unclear.
09.04.25 14:20
Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity
10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Synchronized On-Prem group:

11.04.25 08:30

• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

# Azure AD Connect / Microsoft Graph group-sync: Engineer-led RCA and remediation

The available records describe a single, focused post-incident analysis where SYSTEM ENGINEER 14 took ownership of a group-membership synchronization issue between on-premises AD and Azure AD. The engineer used Azure AD Connect synchronization context to reproduce and explain the problem and relied on Microsoft Graph queries to enumerate and compare cloud-native and synchronized on-prem group membership. The engineer's work included in-depth analysis, reproduction in test, identification of root cause, and guidance on remediation actions described as consolidation, cleanup and a full sync.

## Clear primary analyst: SYSTEM ENGINEER 14 led the investigation and remediation

SYSTEM ENGINEER 14 is identified as the engineer who took over the ticket for in‑depth analysis, reproduced the issue in test, identified the root cause, and guided remediation actions (consolidation, cleanup, full sync).

This establishes a single technical owner for the investigative work and immediate remediation guidance, which is useful for post‑incident follow up, knowledge capture, and accountability for the changes applied. [Data: Entities (261)]

## Azure AD Connect used as the synchronization context to reproduce and explain the issue

The engineer specifically reviewed Azure AD Connect behavior and used its synchronization context to reproduce and explain the issue, indicating the problem is rooted in or observable through the AD Connect sync pipeline.

This means troubleshooting included examining the sync engine's view of objects and likely timing/state of sync cycles rather than only cloud-side artifacts, which has implications for remediation (e.g., performing full syncs, addressing on‑prem object state, or connector configuration). [Data: Relationships (430)]

## Microsoft Graph employed to enumerate and compare group membership

Microsoft Graph was used as the audit/enumeration mechanism to review group membership and to compare cloud-native and synchronized on-prem group member lists.

Using Graph queries to enumerate members demonstrates the investigative approach relied on cloud API-derived inventories for validation and comparison, which supports precise verification of remediation outcomes and can feed automated checks or runbooks. [Data: Entities (271); Relationships (431)]

## Investigation workflow combined on‑prem sync context with cloud membership queries

The documented relationships show the engineer bridged Azure AD Connect (sync context) analysis and Microsoft Graph (cloud membership enumeration) to reproduce and explain the issue.

That combined approach is appropriate for group membership sync problems: Azure AD Connect provides the sync pipeline and source-of-truth context, while Graph shows the effective cloud state. It also implies both on‑prem and cloud views were necessary to identify divergence and validate remediation. [Data: Entities (261, 271); Relationships (430, 431)]

## Remediation steps referenced: consolidation, cleanup, and a full sync

The engineer's notes explicitly reference remediation actions described as consolidation, cleanup, and a full sync as part of guided remediation.

These steps are consistent with resolving membership divergence—consolidation/cleanup addresses duplicate or stale objects and membership, while a full sync forces reconciliation; documenting these as the chosen actions is important for runbook capture and audit trails. [Data: Entities (261)]

## Operational implication: coordination needed between directory owners and sync operators

Because the analysis required both the AD Connect sync context and Microsoft Graph enumeration, successful remediation and future prevention will require coordinated activities across on‑prem AD owners, Azure AD/sync operators, and whoever owns group membership policies.

This coordination covers agreeing remediation windows for a full sync, communicating membership consolidation to application owners, and ensuring verification steps post‑sync are performed. The records indicate a cross‑boundary troubleshooting approach that should be formalized into escalation and runbook steps. [Data: Entities (261, 271); Relationships (430, 431)]

## Governance and runbook gap: evidence of engineer-driven, ad-hoc remediation

The available notes show SYSTEM ENGINEER 14 performed in‑depth analysis and guided remediation actions, which suggests the fix was engineer-driven rather than executed via an established automated runbook or formally scheduled change.

This pattern points to an opportunity to capture the steps used (Graph queries, Azure AD Connect checks, consolidation criteria, verification queries, and the full sync procedure) into an operational runbook and change template so future occurrences can be resolved with lower risk and clearer owner handoffs. Capturing these artifacts will improve repeatability and auditability. [Data: Entities (261, 271); Relationships (430, 431)]

question: Is the statement supported by the evidence?
answer: Multiple Scan‑to‑Mail incidents were investigated by reviewing SMTP relay logs; two decisive relay log messages were (a) “MESSAGE SIZE EXCEEDS FIXED LIMIT” (oversized PDF rejection) and (b) “Relay denied” (printer IP/VLAN not in relay allow‑list). In those incidents printers showed local “Send successful” while the relay rejected the message (TI‑1624682 and TI‑1624175) [Documented (records)]

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail fails for larger documents
Ticket number: TI-1624682
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 28
Owner: Employee 34
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 15.01.26 08:57
Closure
Closure date: 23.01.26 15:36
Closure text: SMTP and mail size limit adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail stable.
Activities
15.01.26 08:45 – Email from Contact Person 28
Good morning
We have noticed that larger scanned documents are not delivered via email.
Small documents (1–2 pages) work normally.
Larger PDFs (approx. 20–30 pages) are not delivered internally or externally.
No clear error message appears on the printer.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
15.01.26 08:57
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 34.
15.01.26 09:30 – Initial analysis
Test scan with one page → delivery successful.
Test scan with 25 pages → no delivery.
Printer displays “Send successful”, but no email in inbox.
15.01.26 10:20 – Mail log review
Internal mail server logs show no incoming message for large files.
SMTP relay log shows entry “Message size exceeds fixed limit”.
16.01.26 09:05 – Inquiry to customer
Were there any recent changes to the mail environment or security policies?
16.01.26 10:10 – Customer response
Two weeks ago, the maximum email size was reduced to limit spam.
We were not aware that this could impact the printers.
17.01.26 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail defines:
• Sending occurs via dedicated SMTP relay
• Maximum file size must be aligned with mail infrastructure
• Changes to mail server policies must be coordinated with scan systems
Finding:
New limit = 10 MB
Scanned PDFs exceed this limit at higher resolution.
20.01.26 – Further analysis
Scan settings on device checked.
Default resolution 600 dpi.
PDF compression disabled.
21.01.26 – Test phase
Temporary reduction of scan resolution to 300 dpi.
Test scan with 25 pages → successful.
Parallel coordination with mail team.
Maximum size moderately increased.
22.01.26 – Configuration adjustment
Scan profile adjusted according to blueprint:
• Default resolution reduced
• PDF compression enabled
• Mail limit adjusted
23.01.26 14:50 – Customer feedback
Several larger documents tested.
Internal and external delivery successful.
23.01.26 15:36 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 28
The root cause was a discrepancy between the scan configuration and the reduced maximum email size in the SMTP relay.
The settings were adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works reliably, including for larger documents.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

=== CITED COMMUNITY REPORTS ===
REPORT 11 [resolved-as: community_report]:
# Scan-to-Mail Community — SMTP Relay, Multifunction Devices, Printer VLAN

This community centers on the Scan-to-Mail service implemented by multifunction printers that send scanned documents via a dedicated SMTP RELAY. The SMTP RELAY enforces an allow-list of source IP ranges (printer VLAN) and a maximum message-size limit (recently reduced to ~10 MB), and it requires authentication by a SERVICE ACCOUNT and TLS. Large, multi-page high-resolution scans (default 600 dpi, PDF compression disabled) exceeded the relay limit and were rejected with explicit log entries ("MESSAGE SIZE EXCEEDS FIXED LIMIT" / "Relay denied"). Internal routing allowed delivery to internal mailboxes, but external forwarding failed until the MAIL TEAM updated the relay allow-list for the printer VLAN and moderately increased the size limit while support adjusted device scan profiles (reduced to 300 dpi and enabled compression). Troubleshooting and remediation required coordinated activity across Support (ticket owners and engineers), Network, and Mail teams and included temporary role/MFA changes for the service account to complete connector operations and testing to external recipients (Gmail, partner). [Data: Entities (136,656,659,135,658), Relationships (214,1135,1140,1128,1139)]

## SMTP RELAY is the single choke point controlling external Scan-to-Mail delivery

The dedicated SMTP RELAY is the central infrastructure component that authenticates devices, enforces TLS, validates source IP ranges, and applies a maximum message-size limit; oversized messages and unallowed source IP ranges caused outbound scan deliveries to be rejected at this component. This is documented in the relay description and was the primary functional bottleneck during incidents. [Data: Entities (136,658), Relationships (1135,1140,214)]

Because the relay sits between multifunction devices and external recipients, changes to its policy (for spam mitigation or allow‑lists) directly affect business workflows like Scan-to-Mail; this dependency was confirmed by successful post‑remediation tests to external providers (Gmail, partner) after relay configuration changes. Operational ownership of these controls rests with the MAIL TEAM, who implemented and later adjusted the size limit and allow-list. [Data: Entities (142,136,143,144), Relationships (1142,218,219)]

## Relay log entries provided clear, reproducible diagnostics that localized failures to the SMTP RELAY

Support analysis identified specific SMTP log entries such as "MESSAGE SIZE EXCEEDS FIXED LIMIT" which explicitly indicate that the relay rejected oversized messages rather than the internal mail server or printer device failing silently. These log entries were used as the primary diagnostic evidence during investigation. [Data: Entities (663,657), Relationships (1139,1146)]

Internal mail server logs corroborated the diagnosis by showing no incoming messages for the failed scans, which demonstrates that the relay rejection occurred before forwarding to internal mail infrastructure. This separation is important operationally because it focuses remediation on relay policy and message sizing rather than internal mailbox processing. [Data: Entities (654), Relationships (1138)]

## Printer VLAN allow-list misconfiguration blocked external delivery while internal delivery remained functional

The multifunction device was placed in a newly defined PRINTER VLAN; the SMTP RELAY’s allow-list did not include that VLAN’s address range, producing "Relay denied" behavior for external messages even though internal routing allowed local delivery. This explains the symptom where the device indicated "Send successful" but external recipients never received messages. [Data: Entities (135,662,136), Relationships (210,214,1150)]

Remediation required updating the relay configuration to include the printer VLAN so devices could be accepted as valid submitters for outbound relay. This change underlines a recurring operational risk when VLAN or IP reassignments occur without parallel relay policy updates. [Data: Relationships (214)]

## Device scan profile (resolution/compression) was a root contributor to oversized messages; profile changes restored large-scan delivery

The devices used a default scan profile of 600 dpi with PDF compression disabled, producing large multi‑page PDFs that exceeded the relay’s 10 MB limit; reducing resolution to 300 dpi and enabling PDF compression produced smaller files that successfully transmitted in tests. This confirms that both infrastructure limits and endpoint behavior jointly determine Scan-to-Mail reliability. [Data: Entities (659,660,661,655), Relationships (1147,1148,1149,1130)]

Operationally, this means remediation may be twofold: (a) adjust relay size policy to tolerate legitimate business use-cases, and (b) standardize device scan profiles (e.g., lower default dpi, enable compression) in the Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard to limit recurrence. The ticket records show support executed device changes and tests (one‑page and 25‑page scans) to validate the fix. [Data: Relationships (1128,1130)]

## Remediation required cross-team coordination and temporary privilege/MFA changes for service account operations

Resolution involved coordinated activity between Support (ticket owners and engineers), Network, and the MAIL TEAM to update relay allow-lists and size limits, as well as device profile changes and testing to external addresses. The tickets (TI-1624175, TI-1624682) document these coordinated steps and closure notes citing adherence to the Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. [Data: Entities (132,652,142), Relationships (197,1123,1142)]

To perform connector reinstallation/operation one time, the SERVICE ACCOUNT was temporarily granted the INTUNE ADMIN ROLE and excluded from MFA, indicating operational shortcuts required to complete changes; these actions were reversed after remediation, but they represent an elevated-privilege path that needs governance. [Data: Entities (106,645), Relationships (1120,1119)]

## Device feedback ("Send successful") is misleading without relay/queue visibility and contributed to delayed detection

Multifunction devices reported a local confirmation message ("Send successful") immediately after submission, but the SMTP RELAY later rejected large messages; the device confirmation thus gave a false positive, delaying detection until logs or recipient complaints surfaced. Support notes explicitly document this disparity. [Data: Entities (662,663), Relationships (1150,1139)]

Operational runbooks should require immediate mail-queue and relay‑log checks when devices report success but recipients report non‑receipt, because endpoint success state does not guarantee end-to-end delivery in relay‑mediated flows. [Data: Entities (727), Relationships (1307)]

## DNS/TLS and external mail security changes were investigated as potential contributors and should be part of pre-change impact assessments

Customer-reported changes to DNS forwarding and an external mail security solution were reviewed during the investigation to ensure they did not cause the failure; TLS configuration is also required for Scan-to-Mail connections. These elements can interact with relay behavior or external filtering and therefore merit inclusion in change impact analysis for mail and scanning infrastructure. [Data: Entities (138,139,725), Relationships (223,1305)]

Including DNS/TLS and external mail-security checks in the Print & Scanning change checklist reduces the chance that unrelated changes will cause delivery regressions; tickets reference these checks in their investigation steps. [Data: Relationships (223,1305,1146)]

## Operational and security gap: relay policy changes for spam control vs. business needs require formal governance and monitoring

The MAIL TEAM reduced the maximum email size as a spam mitigation step and later had to moderate the limit to restore business scanning functionality. While anti‑spam controls are important, the unilateral application of a global size limit without exception handling for sanctioned device classes (printer VLAN / Scan-to-Mail) created an availability impact to business workflows. The ticket trail records the initial reduction and the later coordination to adjust the limit. [Data: Entities (664,658,142), Relationships (1142,1135)]

Additionally, temporary elevation of service account privileges and exclusion from MFA created short-term compliance exposures; these actions were necessary to complete connector operations but underscore the need for documented privileged-operation runbooks, time-bound approvals, logging, and post‑change verification to avoid policy drift. Implementing exception handling for sanctioned devices and alerting on relay rejection patterns will reduce both risk and time to remediation. [Data: Entities (106,645), Relationships (1120,1119)]

Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 28
Owner: Employee 34
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 15.01.26 08:57
Closure
Closure date: 23.01.26 15:36
Closure text: SMTP and mail size limit adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail stable.
Activities
15.01.26 08:45 – Email from Contact Person 28
Good morning
We have noticed that larger scanned documents are not delivered via email.
Small documents (1–2 pages) work normally.
Larger PDFs (approx. 20–30 pages) are not delivered internally or externally.
No clear error message appears on the printer.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
15.01.26 08:57
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 34.
15.01.26 09:30 – Initial analysis
Test scan with one page → delivery successful.
Test scan with 25 pages → no delivery.
Printer displays “Send successful”, but no email in inbox.
15.01.26 10:20 – Mail log review
Internal mail server logs show no incoming message for large files.
SMTP relay log shows entry “Message size exceeds fixed limit”.
16.01.26 09:05 – Inquiry to customer
Were there any recent changes to the mail environment or security policies?
16.01.26 10:10 – Customer response
Two weeks ago, the maximum email size was reduced to limit spam.
We were not aware that this could impact the printers.
17.01.26 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail defines:
• Sending occurs via dedicated SMTP relay
• Maximum file size must be aligned with mail infrastructure
• Changes to mail server policies must be coordinated with scan systems
Finding:
New limit = 10 MB
Scanned PDFs exceed this limit at higher resolution.
20.01.26 – Further analysis
Scan settings on device checked.
Default resolution 600 dpi.
PDF compression disabled.
21.01.26 – Test phase
Temporary reduction of scan resolution to 300 dpi.
Test scan with 25 pages → successful.
Parallel coordination with mail team.
Maximum size moderately increased.
22.01.26 – Configuration adjustment
Scan profile adjusted according to blueprint:
• Default resolution reduced
• PDF compression enabled
• Mail limit adjusted
23.01.26 14:50 – Customer feedback
Several larger documents tested.
Internal and external delivery successful.
23.01.26 15:36 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 28
The root cause was a discrepancy between the scan configuration and the reduced maximum email size in the SMTP relay.
The settings were adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works reliably, including for larger documents.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail works only for internal recipients
Ticket number: TI-1624175
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 25
Owner: Employee 30
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 10.12.25 08:41
Closure
Closure date: 18.12.25 16:04
Closure text: SMTP relay and DNS configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail fully restored.
Activities
10.12.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 25
Good morning
At the Zurich location, we can scan from the multifunction device, but external recipients (e.g., Gmail or partner companies) do not receive the emails.
Internal recipients within our company receive the scans normally.
Printing continues to function without issue.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
10.12.25 08:41
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 30.
10.12.25 09:15 – Initial analysis
Test scan to internal mailbox → successful.
Test scan to external address → no delivery.
No error message visible on the printer itself.
10.12.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Have there been any recent changes to the mail environment or network?
10.12.25 10:40 – Customer response
Last week, the external mail security solution was adjusted.
Additionally, new DNS forwarding was configured at the location.
11.12.25 09:10 – Mail log analysis
Internal delivery occurs directly.
External delivery is not forwarded by the SMTP relay.
SMTP log shows: “Relay denied”.
11.12.25 11:30 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail:
• Printers must not send directly to the internet
• Sending occurs exclusively via dedicated SMTP relay
• Relay allows only defined IP ranges
• DNS resolution must occur internally
• Authentication must not be bypassed
12.12.25 – Further analysis
Check of printer IP address.
Printer is located in newly defined printer VLAN.
This VLAN is not configured as an allowed range in the SMTP relay.
Internal routing works, but external relay is blocked.
15.12.25 – Coordination with Network and Mail Team
Confirmation that the new printer VLAN was not included in the relay allow list.
16.12.25 – Adjustment
SMTP relay configuration updated according to blueprint.
IP range of printer VLAN released.
17.12.25 – Test phase
Scan to internal mailbox → successful
Scan to external Gmail address → successful
Scan to partner company → successful
18.12.25 16:04 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 25
The root cause was an SMTP relay configuration that had not been updated after the introduction of a new printer VLAN.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works for both internal and external recipients.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF52: Microsoft Teams – No audio in external calls
Ticket number: TI-1602241
Company: Customer Company 52 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 14
Owner: Employee 33
Origin: Phone
Type: Incident
Created on: 08.07.25 08:21
Closure
Closure date: 18.07.25 16:05
Closure text: Configuration adjusted according to Direct Routing architecture. Voice transmission stable again.
Activities
08.07.25 08:10
Call from Mr. Contact Person 14.
He reports that several employees since this morning cannot hear audio during external phone calls in Microsoft Teams. The call is established, the external participant can hear the employees, but they themselves hear nothing. It is described as “completely silent”.
Internal Teams calls function normally. Chats and video conferences are also not affected.
08.07.25 08:21
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33.
08.07.25 09:05 – Email from Employee 33 to Contact Person 14
Good morning Mr. Contact Person 14
Thank you for your report.
Could you please briefly confirm the following points:
– Does it affect only external calls (mobile/fixed line)?
– Are both incoming and outgoing calls affected?
– Does the issue occur for all users or only specific ones?
– Since when has the issue existed?
Thank you for your feedback.
Kind regards
08.07.25 09:34 – Response from Contact Person 14
Good morning
Yes, it affects only external calls.
Both incoming and outgoing.
At the moment, about a dozen people are affected, all at the Zurich location.
Since this morning, everything was normal yesterday.
Kind regards
08.07.25 10:12 – Internal note
Test call performed with user S. Meier.
Internal Teams call works.
External call to mobile phone: connection established, conversation partner confirms he can hear Mr. Meier, Mr. Meier hears nothing.
Teams shows status “Connected”. No error message.
08.07.25 11:40 – Email to customer
We have restarted the Teams client and cleared the cache as a test.
Could you please check with two additional affected users whether the behavior is identical?
08.07.25 13:05 – Customer response
We tested with three additional persons.
Same behavior everywhere.
Headsets differ (Jabra, Poly).
LAN connection.
Home office users do not seem to be affected.
09.07.25 08:50 – Customer contacts again
Good morning
We just remembered that a firewall adjustment (security hardening) was carried out over the weekend.
Could this have an impact?
Kind regards
09.07.25 09:30 – Internal note
Firewall change timing plausible.
Escalated to Unified Communications / Network team.
09.07.25 10:22 – Ticket taken over by System Engineer 18
09.07.25 11:15 – Email from System Engineer 18 to customer
Good day
We are currently analyzing the Direct Routing configuration.
Signaling (SIP) appears to function correctly, as calls can be established.
We are currently checking the media flow (audio stream).
We will get back to you as soon as we have further findings.
Kind regards
10.07.25 08:40 – Internal analysis
SBC shows successful SIP connections.
No call drops.
Media negotiation (SDP) is performed.
Notable:
RTP traffic appears stable only in one direction.
10.07.25 10:50 – Firewall rule comparison
New rule implemented at Zurich location.
Outbound traffic configured more restrictively.
Comparison with Microsoft documentation shows deviation in media port ranges.
10.07.25 14:15 – Internal test phase
Temporary adjustment of firewall configuration for media ports.
Test call performed.
Audio immediately available in both directions.
Firewall reverted to restrictive rule.
Issue occurs again.
11.07.25 09:05 – Email to customer
Good day
We have identified a correlation with a recently implemented firewall rule at the Zurich location.
Call signaling functions correctly; however, media traffic is partially blocked.
We are currently reviewing the configuration in detail according to our Direct Routing architecture specification.
We will provide an update shortly.
Kind regards
14.07.25 – Internal documentation review
Comparison with internal document
“Blueprint – Microsoft Teams Direct Routing & Media Flow – Version 2.1”
Particularly relevant: section regarding location configuration with Media Bypass enabled.
Finding:
Zurich location configured with Media Bypass.
Firewall rule considers only signaling ports, not full media port range.
16.07.25 – Configuration adjustment
Firewall rule corrected according to architectural specification.
Details not documented in ticket for security reasons.
17.07.25 – Customer feedback
Good morning
We tested several external calls.
Audio works normally again.
Longer conversations are also stable.
Thank you for your support.
Kind regards
18.07.25 16:05 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 14
The root cause was a restriction of media traffic at the Zurich location following a firewall adjustment.
The configuration was corrected according to the internal Direct Routing architecture specification.
Voice transmission is now stable again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – External project members lose access sporadically
Ticket number: TI-1583327
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 07
Owner: Employee 31
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 07.04.25 08:12
Closure
Closure date: 17.04.25 15:18
Closure text: Root cause identified (Azure AD Connect synchronization error affecting group attributes). Cleanup and full sync performed. Access stable.
Activities
07.04.25 08:04
Subject: Project members (external) lose access to SharePoint
From: Contact Person 07
Good morning
Several external project members report that they no longer have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Internal employees do not appear to have issues.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 07
07.04.25 08:12
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 31
Status: In Progress
Notes
07.04.25 09:05
Helpdesk asks:
• Does it affect only external users?
• What error message appears?
• Since when exactly?
• Were groups or permissions adjusted?
07.04.25 10:20
Customer responds:
Error message:
Access denied
One user could access yesterday.
Today no longer.
No deliberate changes made.
07.04.25 11:10
Helpdesk checks SharePoint:
• Folder permissions correct
• Group “SP_Compliance_Main” contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• External users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
Comparison internal vs external users:
No visible difference.
08.04.25 08:45
Customer adds:
One external user was newly added and immediately had access.
Another who has been in the project for months loses access sporadically.
08.04.25 10:15
Helpdesk tests:
• Remove + re-add to group
→ Access temporarily restored
After approx. 1 hour, no access again.
08.04.25 14:30
Hypothesis:
Token / cache / Conditional Access
Test:
User fully logs out → logs in again
→ Access remains denied
Hypothesis rejected.
09.04.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are external users created as “Guest” or “Member”?
09.04.25 10:05
Customer:
All externals are created as Guest.
Some were recently synchronized from On-Prem AD to Cloud.
09.04.25 11:40
First observation:
For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute
“source” = Windows Server AD
For functioning external users:
“source” = Azure AD
Correlation unclear.
09.04.25 14:20
Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity
10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Owner: Employee 33
Origin: Telephone

Closure text: Microphone not properly connected. Device configured correctly. Function restored.
Activities
14.10.25 09:10 – Telephone report
Mr. Contact Person 07 reports that an employee can hear everything during Teams calls but cannot be heard by the other participant.
Both internal and external calls are affected.
Chat works.
Camera works.
14.10.25 09:18
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 33.
14.10.25 09:25 – Helpdesk inquiry
Good morning
Could you please briefly confirm:
– Is a headset or the integrated microphone being used?
– Does the issue also occur during a Teams test call?
Thank you.
14.10.25 09:32 – Customer response
A USB headset is being used.
No sound is recorded during the test call either.
14.10.25 09:40 – Remote session
Connected with the user.
In Teams, “USB Audio Device” is selected as the microphone.
However, the input level indicator remains empty.
In the Windows Sound menu, no input signal is displayed either.
After a brief check, it was determined that although the USB headset was plugged in, the microphone cable on the headset itself was not fully inserted.
After correctly plugging it in, the input level indicator immediately showed activity.

14.10.25 09:50 – User feedback
Call tested. Working properly again.
14.10.25 09:52 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The microphone was not properly connected, which prevented any input signal from being detected.
After correctly reconnecting the device, voice transmission is functioning normally again.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

04.03.25 14:05 – Initial analysis

The root cause was not group nesting, but a delay in Conditional Access policy evaluation combined with cached authentication tokens.

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF60: Scan-to-Mail works only for internal recipients
Ticket number: TI-1624175
Company: Customer Company 60 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 25
Owner: Employee 30
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 10.12.25 08:41
Closure
Closure date: 18.12.25 16:04
Closure text: SMTP relay and DNS configuration adjusted according to Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard. Scan-to-Mail fully restored.
Activities
10.12.25 08:30 – Email from Contact Person 25
Good morning
At the Zurich location, we can scan from the multifunction device, but external recipients (e.g., Gmail or partner companies) do not receive the emails.
Internal recipients within our company receive the scans normally.
Printing continues to function without issue.
Could you please check this?
Kind regards
10.12.25 08:41
Ticket created and assigned to Employee 30.
10.12.25 09:15 – Initial analysis
Test scan to internal mailbox → successful.
Test scan to external address → no delivery.
No error message visible on the printer itself.
10.12.25 10:05 – Inquiry to customer
Have there been any recent changes to the mail environment or network?
10.12.25 10:40 – Customer response
Last week, the external mail security solution was adjusted.
Additionally, new DNS forwarding was configured at the location.
11.12.25 09:10 – Mail log analysis
Internal delivery occurs directly.
External delivery is not forwarded by the SMTP relay.
SMTP log shows: “Relay denied”.
11.12.25 11:30 – Comparison with blueprint
“Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard – Version 1.0”
Section Scan-to-Mail:
• Printers must not send directly to the internet
• Sending occurs exclusively via dedicated SMTP relay
• Relay allows only defined IP ranges
• DNS resolution must occur internally
• Authentication must not be bypassed
12.12.25 – Further analysis
Check of printer IP address.
Printer is located in newly defined printer VLAN.
This VLAN is not configured as an allowed range in the SMTP relay.
Internal routing works, but external relay is blocked.
15.12.25 – Coordination with Network and Mail Team
Confirmation that the new printer VLAN was not included in the relay allow list.
16.12.25 – Adjustment
SMTP relay configuration updated according to blueprint.
IP range of printer VLAN released.
17.12.25 – Test phase
Scan to internal mailbox → successful
Scan to external Gmail address → successful
Scan to partner company → successful
18.12.25 16:04 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 25
The root cause was an SMTP relay configuration that had not been updated after the introduction of a new printer VLAN.
The configuration was adjusted according to the “Print & Scanning Infrastructure Standard”.
Scan-to-Mail now works for both internal and external recipients.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Ticket summary:
Ticket name: KF25: SharePoint – External project members lose access sporadically
Ticket number: TI-1583327
Company: Customer Company 25 AG
Contact person: Contact Person 07
Owner: Employee 31
Origin: Email
Type: Incident
Created on: 07.04.25 08:12
Closure
Closure date: 17.04.25 15:18
Closure text: Root cause identified (Azure AD Connect synchronization error affecting group attributes). Cleanup and full sync performed. Access stable.
Activities
07.04.25 08:04
Subject: Project members (external) lose access to SharePoint
From: Contact Person 07
Good morning
Several external project members report that they no longer have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Internal employees do not appear to have issues.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 07
07.04.25 08:12
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 31
Status: In Progress
Notes
07.04.25 09:05
Helpdesk asks:
• Does it affect only external users?
• What error message appears?
• Since when exactly?
• Were groups or permissions adjusted?
07.04.25 10:20
Customer responds:
Error message:
Access denied
One user could access yesterday.
Today no longer.
No deliberate changes made.
07.04.25 11:10
Helpdesk checks SharePoint:
• Folder permissions correct
• Group “SP_Compliance_Main” contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• External users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
Comparison internal vs external users:
No visible difference.
08.04.25 08:45
Customer adds:
One external user was newly added and immediately had access.
Another who has been in the project for months loses access sporadically.
08.04.25 10:15
Helpdesk tests:
• Remove + re-add to group
→ Access temporarily restored
After approx. 1 hour, no access again.
08.04.25 14:30
Hypothesis:
Token / cache / Conditional Access
Test:
User fully logs out → logs in again
→ Access remains denied
Hypothesis rejected.
09.04.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are external users created as “Guest” or “Member”?
09.04.25 10:05
Customer:
All externals are created as Guest.
Some were recently synchronized from On-Prem AD to Cloud.
09.04.25 11:40
First observation:
For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute
“source” = Windows Server AD
For functioning external users:
“source” = Azure AD
Correlation unclear.
09.04.25 14:20
Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity
10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company

Closure date: 17.04.25 15:18
Closure text: Root cause identified (Azure AD Connect synchronization error affecting group attributes). Cleanup and full sync performed. Access stable.
Activities
07.04.25 08:04
Subject: Project members (external) lose access to SharePoint
From: Contact Person 07
Good morning
Several external project members report that they no longer have access to the folder
/Projects/Compliance_Review_2025
Internal employees do not appear to have issues.
Please review.
Kind regards
Contact Person 07
07.04.25 08:12
Ticket created
Assigned to: Employee 31
Status: In Progress
Notes
07.04.25 09:05
Helpdesk asks:
• Does it affect only external users?
• What error message appears?
• Since when exactly?
• Were groups or permissions adjusted?
07.04.25 10:20
Customer responds:
Error message:
Access denied
One user could access yesterday.
Today no longer.
No deliberate changes made.
07.04.25 11:10
Helpdesk checks SharePoint:
• Folder permissions correct
• Group “SP_Compliance_Main” contains “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
• External users are members of “COMPLIANCE_TEAM”
Comparison internal vs external users:
No visible difference.
08.04.25 08:45
Customer adds:
One external user was newly added and immediately had access.
Another who has been in the project for months loses access sporadically.
08.04.25 10:15
Helpdesk tests:
• Remove + re-add to group
→ Access temporarily restored
After approx. 1 hour, no access again.
08.04.25 14:30
Hypothesis:
Token / cache / Conditional Access
Test:
User fully logs out → logs in again
→ Access remains denied
Hypothesis rejected.
09.04.25 09:12
Helpdesk asks:
Are external users created as “Guest” or “Member”?
09.04.25 10:05
Customer:
All externals are created as Guest.
Some were recently synchronized from On-Prem AD to Cloud.
09.04.25 11:40
First observation:
For two affected users in Entra ID portal, attribute
“source” = Windows Server AD
For functioning external users:
“source” = Azure AD
Correlation unclear.
09.04.25 14:20
Ticket escalated for in-depth analysis.
Assigned Group changed to: BU Infrastructure – Identity
10.04.25 08:55
Ticket taken over by: System Engineer 14
In-depth analysis
10.04.25 09:40
Azure AD Connect reviewed.
Observation:
Group “COMPLIANCE_TEAM” exists both On-Prem and in Cloud.
On-Prem version is synchronized.
Cloud version was originally created manually.
Result:
• External guests exist only in Cloud group
• SharePoint references the synchronized On-Prem group
Same name, but different Object IDs.
10.04.25 11:25
Review of group members via Graph:
Cloud group:
→ Contains external guests
Synchronized On-Prem group:
→ Contains only internal users
Since SharePoint evaluates by Object ID, not by name, external users lose access once synchronization takes effect.
11.04.25 08:30
Reproduction in test:
• On-Prem group re-synchronized
• Cloud group overwritten
• External users reproducibly lose access
Root Cause
Two groups existed with identical name:
COMPLIANCE_TEAM
1. Cloud-native group
2. Synchronized On-Prem group
Through Azure AD Connect, the Cloud group was gradually overwritten or deactivated.
SharePoint referenced the synchronized version.
External guests were never managed On-Prem and therefore were not included in the synchronized group.
Actions
11.04.25 – 14.04.25
• Consolidation of group structure
• Old Cloud group removed
• On-Prem group properly cleaned
• External guests managed as Cloud-only members
• Azure AD Connect full synchronization performed
15.04.25 – Monitoring
Access remains stable
No further inconsistencies
17.04.25 15:18 – Ticket closure
Dear Contact Person 07
The root cause was the parallel existence of two groups with identical names but different origins (Cloud vs. On-Prem synchronized).
Due to synchronization, effective group membership for external users was not correctly applied.
The group structure was consolidated and fully synchronized.
Access is now stable.
Kind regards
Consulting Sample Company
